title: Subqueries, CTEs, Set Operations, and Relational Reasoning · SQL Query Room
summary: Operations needs reusable query stages for backlog comparisons, missing relationships, and cohorts across time windows. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a composable backlog-analysis query pipeline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose scalar, row, and table subqueries whose cardinality matches their surrounding expression.
Distinguish correlated subqueries and use EXISTS or NOT EXISTS for semijoin and antijoin questions.
Use CTEs to name query stages while distinguishing readability from materialization guarantees.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Set Operations
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-set-operations-1smk1p" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; subquery, CTE, recursion, or set-operation evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Set Operations.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Set Operations into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Set Operations, keep Set Operations connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-set-operations-1smk1p
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT teams.name
FROM teams
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM tickets WHERE tickets.team_id = teams.team_id)
ORDER BY teams.name;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
